prompt: Create a CampusStore Inventory & Marketplace Operations platform that lets a school community run an offline, on-prem marketplace and stockroom workflow with strong governance. Users sign in with a local username and password and see role-specific navigation rendered with Thymeleaf templates: Students browse and request items, Teachers act as approvers and occasional requestors, and Administrators manage catalog, locations, policies, and audits. The home experience centers on fast keyword search with tokenized matching, multi-dimensional filters (category, price range in USD, item condition, and campus area), and sorting by newest, price, popularity, and distance using an internal “zone distance” table rather than maps. The UI captures search history on-device/server-side and shows trending terms from the last 7 days, while lightweight personalization boosts results based on the user’s last 30 browsed items and favorites, with an obvious toggle to disable personalization. A profile area allows users to maintain contacts and US-style addresses, review their history of requests and fulfillment, set preferences and tags (for example “lab supplies” or “graduation”), and manage notification settings including Do Not Disturb windows (for example 10:00 PM–7:00 AM). The Reminder & Notification Center generates in-app notifications and optional offline email-to-outbox messages for later export, supporting rules like “pickup overdue by 24 hours” or “missed check-in,” with per-category opt-in/opt-out and a quiet-hours override for critical administrative notices.The backend uses Spring Boot to implement a decoupled REST-style API consumed by the Thymeleaf frontend, enforcing least-privilege access with server-side authorization checks on every request and permission-change tracking tied to an immutable audit log. Order and request data is access-controlled so only the requester, assigned staff, and Administrators can view details; Teachers only see items they approved or are assigned to. Sensitive fields (password hashes, contact phone numbers, addresses) are encrypted at rest using AES-256 with keys stored locally, and all HTTP traffic is forced over local TLS; UI masking shows only the last 4 digits of phone numbers. Rate control blocks scraping by limiting anonymous endpoints to 30 requests/minute and authenticated users to 120 requests/minute, with escalating lockouts (5 minutes after 10 violations). MySQL persists users, roles, tags, favorites, search logs, inventory, locations, and pick tasks; data retention defaults to 365 days for operational logs and 7 years for audits, with compliant deletion workflows that hard-delete disabled/blacklisted user profiles after 30 days unless linked to an open dispute. Crawl observability is implemented as an internal “crawler job” runner for local data sources (files or intranet pages), tracking success rate, latency, parse hit rate, and anti-bot blocks; when thresholds are breached (for example success rate < 95% over 1 hour or latency p95 > 2.0 seconds), the system raises an in-app alert to Administrators and automatically snapshots the failed sample (raw content, parser version, timestamp) for offline troubleshooting. The Intelligent Putaway and Picking Strategy Center stores location attributes (temperature zone, security level, capacity), supports ABC classification, FIFO/expiration rules, and heat scoring from the last 14 days of picks; it recommends putaway bins and generates pick paths using a configurable path-cost matrix, with a simulation mode that compares strategies against historical orders and reports estimated steps saved and completion time without relying on any external services.
